name: Port de Mont-ral Loop from Valls
name_original: Valls - Alcover - Mont-ral - La Riba – vuelta por el Alt Camp
description: This difficult 31.9-mile road cycling route from Valls explores the Alt Camp region, climbing 3001 feet through diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Road cycling around Valls, a municipality in Tarragona, Spain, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region features a mix of challenging climbs and moderate routes, often on mostly paved surfaces, with significant elevation gains in some areas. The landscape is characterized by hills and mountains, providing panoramic views and varied cycling experiences. Cyclists can expect a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with routes often featuring rolling hills and scenic vistas. Best road cycling routes around Valls The most popular road cycling route is Valls - Alcover - Mont-ral - La Riba – return through the Alt Camp, a 31.9 miles (51.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses diverse terrain with notable elevation changes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alt de la Mussara – Port de Mont-ral loop from Valls, a difficult 41.3 miles (66.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and offers panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo del Paborde – Plaça del Mercadal, Reus loop from Valls, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through rolling hills and past cultural landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours. Road cycling around Valls is defined by hilly and mountainous terrain, significant elevation gains, and scenic vistas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Valls are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 2,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valls's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Valls?
A: There are over 70 road cycling routes around Valls, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult rides to explore the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Valls?
A: The months of April and May are particularly popular for road cycling in the Valls area. The weather during this period is generally favorable for exploring the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Valls?
A: Road cycling routes in Valls feature a diverse mix of terrain. You can expect rolling hills, scenic vistas, and significant elevation gains, especially on routes leading to higher points like Alt de la Mussara. While mostly paved, some sections might include varied surfaces, traversing through farmlands and wooded areas.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Valls?
A: Yes, Valls offers challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39546473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de la Mussara – Coll de Capafonts loop from Valls&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, exceeding 1400 meters, and require very good fitness. Other notable climbs include Alt de la Mussara and Torre de la Mixarda.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Valls?
A: The road cycling routes in Valls are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain passes to scenic rides through farmlands, and the panoramic views offered along many routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Valls?
A: While many routes in Valls feature significant elevation, there are 6 easy road cycling routes available. These routes are generally shorter and have less demanding climbs, making them more suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. Look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances.

Q: Can I find loop routes for road cycling in Valls?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Valls are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38286292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de la Mussara – Port de Mont-ral loop from Valls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10428186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Paborde – Plaça del Mercadal, Reus loop from Valls&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along road cycling routes in Valls?
A: Many routes pass by historical and natural landmarks. You can encounter impressive mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Mont-ral&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3310183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Coloma Pass&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, some routes lead past historic castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5243585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Paborde&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/745758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prenafeta Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the hilly and mountainous terrain around Valls provides numerous scenic viewpoints. Routes that ascend to higher elevations, such as those towards Alt de la Mussara, offer panoramic views of the surrounding area and diverse landscapes, including farmlands and wooded regions.

Q: Is it possible to find parking for cyclists in Valls?
A: Valls, as a municipality, generally offers various parking options. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the starting points on komoot for details on available parking facilities, which are often found near town centers or trailheads.

Q: Can I use public transport to access road cycling routes in Valls?
A: Valls is connected by public transport, which can be used to reach the area. However, specific information regarding the transport of bicycles on local buses or trains should be verified with the respective transport providers, as policies can vary.

Q: Are there cafes or pubs suitable for cyclists in Valls?
A: Valls and the surrounding small communities along the routes offer various cafes and pubs where cyclists can stop for refreshments. Many local establishments are accustomed to catering to outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that are considered 'hidden gems' or less crowded?
A: While popular routes like the Alt de la Mussara are well-known, the region has 72 routes in total. Exploring some of the moderate or less-traveled paths, especially those through farmlands and wooded areas away from major climbs, can offer a more tranquil and less crowded cycling experience.
tips: TV-7041 from Alcover to Mont-ral. Average slope about 6%. Good curves and good scenery between two charming towns.

This mountain road passes through the villages of La Riba, El Pinetell, and Farena, alongside the Brugent River. It's a peaceful route with beautiful views. In the shaded area, there is a risk of frost in winter. The road has good asphalt and two lanes from La Riba to Farena, with gradients between 8 and 12% and some flat sections and descents between El Pinetell and Farena. From Farena onwards, the road narrows to a single lane, but the asphalt remains good, with gradients exceeding 15%. The road joins the TV-7041 from Mont-ral to Capafonts at La Cadeneta.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated moving time for this route is around 2 hours and 40 minutes. However, plan for a longer overall duration to account for stops, enjoying the scenery, and any breaks you might need, especially given its challenging nature.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and difficulty like for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route, categorized as 'difficult' and requiring 'very good fitness'. You'll encounter varied and demanding terrain, including significant elevation gains and descents as you traverse or skirt the Prades Mountains. Expect average gradients around 4%, with some ramps reaching 10% or even higher, particularly on the ascents towards Mont-ral.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, from extensive olive groves and agricultural fields around Valls and Alcover to rugged, mountainous terrain with forests and impressive rock formations as you climb towards Mont-ral. Key highlights include the challenging climb over Port de Mont-ral and the scenic La Riba–Farena Mountain Road. You'll also pass through charming small villages with historical architecture.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While the route is a loop, Valls is a common starting point. You can look for parking options within Valls, which is the largest town on the route and well-connected. Specific trailhead parking isn't designated as it's a road cycling loop, so street parking or public car parks in Valls would be your best bet.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or is it more for experienced cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' due to its significant elevation gain and demanding terrain. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists looking for a challenging ride rather than beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Alt Camp region generally offers good cycling conditions outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn are ideal, providing pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Avoid the peak summer heat, especially on the climbs, and be prepared for cooler, potentially wetter conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find amenities in the towns along the route. Valls, Alcover, Mont-ral, and La Riba all offer opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Mont-ral, being a smaller mountain village, might have fewer options but still provides local charm. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known cycling or walking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several official trails. You'll find it crosses small sections of paths like El Brogit de la Vall, Sender Albiol - Cogullons, El Camí IT6TR5 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, and Ruta de les Muralles, among others. These are mostly minor overlaps as it's primarily a road cycling route.

faqs.question: Is the Valls - Alcover - Mont-ral - La Riba route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it's not typically considered 'dog-friendly' in the same way a hiking trail might be. While you might pass through areas where dogs are allowed on leash, bringing a dog along for the entire 51 km road ride is generally not recommended for their safety or comfort. If you plan stops, check local regulations for pets in towns.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road route through the Alt Camp region. You are free to enjoy the roads and natural beauty without any special permissions.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see on this ride?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse and beautiful landscape. The ride starts and ends in the agricultural plains of Alt Camp with olive groves, transitioning to more rugged, forested, and mountainous terrain as you ascend towards Mont-ral. The area is known for its well-preserved Mediterranean forests, dramatic valleys, and charming villages, offering stunning views throughout.
